danger_signals.visual_form: Jaws' dorsal fin breaking the water surface on overhead map
danger_signals.meaning: Immediate threat—Jaws is present and will collide with boat, forcing diver into combat

danger_signals.visual_form: Audio cue (beeping speed increases) from tracking device
danger_signals.meaning: Proximity warning—Jaws is getting closer to player's boat

danger_signals.visual_form: Jellyfish, rays, smaller sharks appearing in side-view undersea encounters
danger_signals.meaning: Environmental danger—collision causes diver death, power loss, item loss

danger_signals.visual_form: Audio signature in 'You've Hit Something!' track
danger_signals.meaning: Threat acknowledgment—collision with obstacle or enemy occurred

safety_signals.meaning: Safe zones for equipment upgrades and currency exchange
Resource acquisition—currency for purchasing safety upgrades
Movement speed increase—tactical advantage for evasion

safety_signals.visual_form: Random spawning submarine unit on map
safety_signals.meaning: Temporary protection—alternate combat form with reduced inertia and torpedo/depth charge weapons
Score accumulation toward extra life (every 30,000 points)

faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Overhead map shows boat position; ports serve as allied locations

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Changes visual representation from dorsal fin (map view) to full creature in side-view combat to close-up first-person perspective during final encounter
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Appears randomly on overhead map; receiver device tracks relative location

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Various sea creature sprites (jellyfish, rays, smaller sharks)
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Different creature types with distinct sprites; jellyfish notably reappear in bonus stage with Galaga-like formation patterns
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Randomly encountered during diver side-view sequences

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Overhead map view with boat sprite navigating open ocean; no creature contact
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Boat moves freely; receiver device (after first port visit) provides only audio beeping at distance

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Perspective shift to side-view; diver sprite deployed into underwater environment with visible hostile creatures
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player switches from boat control to diver control; diver must combat sea creatures; collision causes instant failure state

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Side-view perspective with large Jaws sprite; smaller visual relative to boat but dominates screen space
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Jaws collision automatic; player forced into combat; health replenishment for Jaws on failed attack

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Shift to first-person perspective from boat bow; Jaws appears as threatening underwater silhouette breaching surface
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player uses strobe device (three charges) to force breach; timing-based jabbing mechanic; Jaws makes random lateral/forward/backward movements

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Bonus stage triggered (after defeating multiple smaller sharks)
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Side-view perspective with airplane sprite traveling horizontally; jellyfish enemies in formation patterns (Galaga-inspired)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player adjusts airplane speed directionally; shooting jellyfish awards conch rewards; perfect clear grants 10,000 bonus points

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Return to overhead map; receiver device disappears from UI; conch shell count reduced by half; health bars reset
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Attack power drops by one level; player must recover and re-engage or visit port for re-equipment

environmental_storytelling.example: Title screen and overwater music swells from soft to loud and back, sonically representing undulating ocean waves and threat proximity through volume dynamics

environmental_storytelling.technique: Dorsal fin as iconic threat symbol
environmental_storytelling.example: Jaws' signature dorsal fin appearance on overhead map visually communicates the specific predator identity without needing detailed creature graphics
Transition from overhead navigation map to side-view combat represents shift from strategic planning to immediate danger; final first-person perspective raises personal stakes
Receiver device (tracks Jaws), upgraded attack power, and submarine unlock represent escalating player capability against increasing threat level
Two alternating ports on map serve as strategic resource nodes—visually separate from ocean, reinforcing sanctuary function
Jellyfish, rays, and smaller sharks populate undersea encounters; reuse of jellyfish in bonus stage suggests they are native fauna, not just random obstacles

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Appears randomly as dorsal fin on map; receiver beeps slowly; erratic spawn timing creates tension
Receiver beeping accelerates; will collide with boat if player does not evade or engage
Guaranteed collision with player's boat; forces diver into water; regains full health if attack fails
Breaches water surface when hit by strobe charge; moves unpredictably left/right/forward; requires precise boat bow timing to deliver killing blow
Randomly appear in encounter zones; do not pursue player visually
Stationary or slowly drifting; collision instant-kills diver; no apparent AI pathing
Can attack sea creatures and Jaws; slow movement; vulnerable to collision
Reduced inertia (faster response); weapons more effective; fragile—one collision destroys unit and releases diver
Free movement in overhead view; subject to collision with obstacles and Jaws
Fixed perspective from bow; player controls jabbing motion; boat bow acts as ramming weapon against breached Jaws

notes: Jaws (NES, 1987) employs a three-perspective visual grammar to communicate escalating danger and player agency: overhead map (strategic/safe), side-view (tactical combat/danger), and first-person bow (climactic vulnerability). The receiver device's beeping serves as primary non-visual danger communication. World-state transitions are marked by perspective shifts rather than environmental transformation. No faction diplomacy or politics present—binary hunter/hunted dynamic. Bonus stage (Galaga-inspired jellyfish level) serves as tension break and resource reward. Music composition uses volume swell dynamics to sonically represent water movement and threat proximity, compensating for NES technical limitations in visual detail. Dorsal fin becomes iconic shorthand for Jaws identity across perspectives.
sources_used: tcrf (The Cutting Room Floor - Jaws NES article)
